cover_story.headline: Erling Haaland Is Building Norway's Boldest Athlete Tech Fund
cover_story.dek: The world's most lethal striker has quietly deployed €50M into Scandinavian health tech and climate infrastructure — and the fund is just getting started.
cover_story.author: Erik Lindström
body: Erling Haaland is 25 years old, has scored 36 Premier League goals in a single season, and remains the most sought-after centre-forward in world football. He is also, quietly, building one of the most focused athlete investment vehicles in European sport. In January 2026, Haaland formalised what had been a series of personal cheques into a structured early-stage fund: Viking Capital, a Bergen-registered vehicle targeting Scandinavian health technology, climate infrastructure, and AI-enabled performance science.

Viking Capital has deployed €50M across seven investments since its Q4 2025 inception. Anchored positions include BioMark, an Oslo-based continuous glucose monitoring platform for elite athletes (€8M Series A); Vayu, a Stockholm climate fintech that converts athlete endorsement fees into renewable energy certificates (€6M seed); and PulseOS, a Copenhagen AI platform for team load management used by four Bundesliga academies. A two-person investment team manages the fund alongside Haaland's family office. Total portfolio AUM stands at €50M with a €100M hard cap target.

Viking Capital is targeting first close at €100M by Q3 2026, with active outreach to three Nordic sovereign wealth funds and two Premier League co-investors. Haaland has told LPs he intends to run the fund throughout his playing career — using his own training load, nutrition protocols, and recovery data as real-world test environments for every portfolio company before writing a cheque.
cover_story.quote: I test every product we invest in during the season. If it doesn't change something measurable in training within 30 days, we don't write the cheque. Full stop.

side_stories.headline: Thierry Henry Leads €15M Series A in European Scouting Platform Pitch
side_stories.body: Arsenal and France legend Thierry Henry has co-led a €15M Series A in Pitch, a Paris-based platform connecting academies, agents, and scouts across Europe's top five leagues. Henry, who has spent years in youth development with Monaco and the French national team, joined as a board observer. Pitch processes 40,000 player profiles across 28 countries.

side_stories.headline: Carlos Sainz Takes Stake in F1 Telemetry Startup Apex Analytics
side_stories.body: Carlos Sainz, in his first season with Williams F1, has taken a strategic equity stake in Apex Analytics, a Madrid-founded platform applying Formula 1 telemetry methodology to commercial fleet optimisation. Sainz joins Apex's advisory board alongside ex-Ferrari chief engineer Enrico Cardile. The company has signed contracts with three European logistics groups.

side_stories.headline: The Athlete Who Waits Until Retirement to Invest Will Always Be Five Years Behind
side_stories.body: The most valuable window for athlete deal flow is the three years before retirement — not after. Your name, your access, and your network peak while you're still competing. Every athlete who launched a fund the day they stopped playing left compounding time on the table.

side_stories.headline: Wout van Aert Backs Belgian E-Bike Platform Velio in €4M Seed
side_stories.body: Belgian cycling superstar Wout van Aert has joined a €4M seed round for Velio, a Ghent-based e-bike subscription platform targeting urban commuters across the Benelux. Van Aert, a three-time Tour de France green jersey winner, called urban mobility the natural intersection between elite cycling and everyday transport. Velio has 8,000 active subscribers.

roundup.headline: Kevin De Bruyne's KDB Studios Closes €20M Series A at €75M Valuation
roundup.body: Belgian midfielder Kevin De Bruyne has closed a €20M Series A for KDB Studios, his Antwerp-registered sports documentary and athlete content production company. The round is led by European media group Mediawan and values KDB Studios at €75M. The company has produced four premium sports documentaries distributed on Prime Video and DAZN since 2024, with a fifth — on the Belgium golden generation — set for release in June 2026.

roundup.headline: Max Verstappen's Real F1 Sim Platform Crosses 500K Paying Subscribers
roundup.body: Max Verstappen's Real F1 — a subscription sim-racing platform co-developed with Red Bull's engineering division — has crossed 500,000 paying subscribers at €14.99 per month, annualising at €90M ARR. The platform delivers AI-generated circuit coaching built from Verstappen's actual onboard telemetry data. Red Bull holds a 25% equity stake in the venture. Real F1 launched in November 2024.

roundup.headline: Israel Adesanya Launches Last Stylebender Streetwear Across Europe
roundup.body: UFC Middleweight legend Israel Adesanya has launched Last Stylebender, a Lagos-and-Auckland co-designed streetwear label, across European markets in April 2026. The brand blends West African textile traditions with MMA aesthetic culture and has secured stockists in 60 stores across the UK, France, and the Netherlands. Adesanya holds full equity and has turned down acquisition approaches from two major European sportswear groups.

roundup.headline: Carlos Alcaraz Backs Spanish Nutrition Brand Fuerza in Seed Round
roundup.body: World number one Carlos Alcaraz has invested an undisclosed seed amount in Fuerza, a Valencia-based sports nutrition brand building recovery products around Mediterranean diet science. Alcaraz joins co-founder and former Olympic swimmer Mireia Belmonte on Fuerza's advisory board. The brand ships to 12 European countries and recorded €3.2M in revenue in its first year of trading.

tips.headline: Validate in Your Home Market First
tips.body: Before you expand a product internationally, prove it in the one market where your name has the most pull. Your home country is your cheapest and highest-converting distribution channel.

tips.headline: Test Products Before You Back Them
tips.body: If you can use the product yourself — in training, recovery, travel — do it before you write a cheque. Haaland deploys portfolio products in his own training load. That due diligence is irreplaceable.

tips.headline: Set a Hard Floor for Your Equity Stake
tips.body: Model dilution before you sign. Three standard funding rounds can take your 40% founding stake to 18%. Know your floor — the minimum ownership that makes the exit worthwhile — before you start fundraising.

tips.headline: Deploy Capital During Season Breaks
tips.body: Pre-season windows and international breaks are when you should be doing due diligence, not just resting. Build your investment pipeline when your competitive schedule is lightest.

tips.headline: Ask for Cohorts, CAC, and Churn First
tips.body: Founders pitch vision. You need data. Before any meeting, request a one-page with 6-month retention, customer acquisition cost, and churn rate. If they can't produce it, that's your answer.

tips.headline: Build Your Board Before You Need to Defend One
tips.body: Every athlete founder needs at least one board member who has run a company at scale. Your Series B investor will ask who's in the room — and 'my agent' is not the right answer.

tips.headline: Your Media Footprint Is a Balance Sheet Item
tips.body: Every major interview, documentary, and branded post compounds your brand equity. Track your media value the way you track revenue — athletes who ignore it negotiate consistently worse commercial deals.

figures.label: Annualised ARR of Verstappen's Real F1 sim platform at 500K paying subscribers — most valuable single athlete-owned digital product in motorsport
Post-money valuation of KDB Studios (Kevin De Bruyne) after €20M Series A led by Mediawan
Viking Capital AUM — Erling Haaland's Scandinavian health and climate tech fund, launched January 2026

investments.headline: Backs Portuguese AI Football Education Platform EduKick
investments.body: Cristiano Ronaldo, through his CR7 Holdings vehicle, has co-invested €8M in EduKick, a Lisbon-founded platform delivering AI-powered coaching curricula to football academies across Portugal, Spain, and Brazil. EduKick uses video analysis and LLM-generated session plans to give amateur coaches access to professional training methodology. The platform has onboarded 2,200 academies since its 2024 launch.

investments.headline: Leads Seed Round in UK Endurance Recovery Platform Seren
investments.body: British triathlete and double Olympic champion Alistair Brownlee has led a £2.5M seed round in Seren, a Cardiff-based platform combining sleep tracking, HRV monitoring, and personalised recovery programming for endurance athletes. Brownlee, who has spoken openly about over-training during his competitive career, said Seren's protocol prevents the burnout cycle that ends amateur athletes' careers prematurely. Seren has 12,000 active users.

investments.headline: Invests in Dutch Sports Mental Health Platform Mindset
investments.body: Dutch international and Arsenal striker Vivianne Miedema has backed Mindset, an Amsterdam-based platform delivering sports psychology and cognitive performance tools to professional football clubs. Miedema, among the first elite athletes to speak publicly about burnout, joined as a brand ambassador and strategic investor. Mindset has signed contracts with 14 clubs across the Eredivisie and the Women's Super League.
